skill.name: stripe-best-practices
skill.description: Best practices for building Stripe integrations, including Checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, Connect platforms, and modern Stripe API choices.
skill.summary: Guide Stripe integration decisions with current API and payment flow recommendations.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown guidance and JSON evaluation artifacts. The URLs are Stripe documentation references, and the flagged cryptography and reconnaissance terms are payment API terminology, not executable behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified As Network Activity
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded Stripe documentation links or text inside evaluation JSON files. No file contains executable HTTP requests or data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain documentation URLs and audit-report text, not code paths that perform network access. No evidence found of credential collection, outbound requests, or hidden endpoints.

low_findings.title: Payment Terms Misclassified As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are triggered by payment and API wording in documentation and JSON reports. No cryptographic implementation appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations discuss Stripe payment APIs, billing, and Connect platform guidance. There is no hashing, cipher selection, cryptographic library use, or custom security primitive.

low_findings.title: API Endpoint Guidance Misclassified As Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings refer to Stripe API guidance and evaluation text. The files do not inspect local systems, enumerate hosts, or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text advises avoiding deprecated Stripe APIs and choosing modern alternatives. No evidence found of probing, local command execution, filesystem inspection, or host discovery.

content.value_statement: Stripe integrations can fail when teams choose outdated APIs or unclear payment flows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward current Stripe patterns.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Checkout Sessions for on-session payments and subscriptions.
Explains when PaymentIntents or SetupIntents are appropriate.
Warns against deprecated Charges, Sources, Tokens, and Card Element patterns.
Guides recurring revenue designs toward Stripe Billing and subscriptions.
Helps choose Connect charge types and account capability concepts.
Points users to relevant Stripe documentation for deeper implementation details.
limitations: Does not execute Stripe API calls or verify live account settings.
Does not replace Stripe documentation for exact parameter requirements.
Does not certify PCI compliance or legal readiness.
Does not debug production payment failures without user-provided context.

use_cases.title: Plan Checkout Implementation
use_cases.description: Choose Checkout Sessions, embedded Checkout, or Payment Element for a store payment flow.

use_cases.title: Design Subscription Billing
use_cases.description: Select Stripe Billing and subscription patterns for recurring revenue products.

use_cases.title: Select Connect Architecture
use_cases.description: Compare direct charges and destination charges for marketplace fund flows.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Payment Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a new Stripe payment flow. Recommend the right Stripe APIs for my use case and explain what to avoid.

prompt_templates.title: Review Checkout Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned Stripe Checkout flow. Check whether I should use hosted Checkout, embedded Checkout, or Payment Element.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Subscription Billing
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design Stripe subscription billing for a SaaS product with trials, upgrades, invoices, and saved payment methods.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Connect Fund Flows
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this Stripe Connect marketplace design. Recommend charge type, liability model, account capability approach, and deprecated concepts to avoid.

output_examples.input: Which Stripe API should I use for a standard online checkout?
output: Use Checkout Sessions for the primary payment flow.
Prefer Stripe-hosted Checkout unless embedded checkout is required.
Use PaymentIntents only when you need more custom payment state.
Do not start a new integration with the Charges API.

output_examples.input: How should I save a customer payment method for later billing?
output: Use SetupIntents to collect and attach the payment method.
Avoid Sources and Tokens for new saved-card designs.
Confirm your PCI scope before handling raw card data server-side.

output_examples.input: What should a marketplace consider before using Stripe Connect?
output: Choose one charge type instead of mixing fund flow models.
Use direct charges when Stripe should manage more risk.
Use destination charges when the platform accepts negative balance liability.
Use controller properties and capabilities instead of old account-type terms.

best_practices: Start with Stripe Checkout before designing a custom payment form.
Use dynamic payment methods instead of hardcoding payment method types.
Map subscription products to Stripe Billing before using direct PaymentIntents.
anti_patterns: Starting new integrations with Charges, Sources, Tokens, or Card Element.
Sending raw PAN data without confirming PCI requirements and Stripe access.
Mixing Connect charge types without a clear liability model.

faq.question: Does this skill write Stripe integration code?
faq.answer: It gives API and architecture guidance. It does not provide a complete implementation by itself.

faq.question: Which Stripe API does it recommend first?
faq.answer: It recommends Checkout Sessions for most on-session payments and many subscription flows.

faq.question: When should I use PaymentIntents?
faq.answer: Use PaymentIntents for off-session payments or when you need to manage payment state directly.

faq.question: Does it support Stripe Billing decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides recurring revenue use cases toward Billing APIs and subscription planning.

faq.question: Does it cover Stripe Connect?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains recommended Connect integration types, charge flows, and account capability concepts.

faq.question: Can it certify my PCI compliance?
faq.answer: No. It can flag Stripe guidance areas, but PCI compliance requires formal review.
